title: Toxic Alcohol Ingestion: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Toxic Alcohol Ingestion
Identify key diagnostic features of Toxic Alcohol Ingestion
Apply clinical reasoning to Toxic Alcohol Ingestion management
Recognize common pitfalls in Toxic Alcohol Ingestion

sections.title: Overview of Toxic Alcohol Ingestion
sections.content:
**Toxic Alcohol Ingestion**

Methanol vs ethylene glycol, osmolal gap, metabolic acidosis with elevated AG, fomepizole

**Key Points:**
• Methanol → formic acid (optic nerve damage, blindness); Ethylene glycol → oxalic acid (renal failure, calcium oxalate crystals)
• Both cause: elevated anion gap metabolic acidosis + elevated osmolal gap
• Early: elevated osmolal gap (parent alcohol); Late: elevated anion gap (toxic metabolites)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Methanol → formic acid (optic nerve damage, blindness); Ethylene glycol → oxalic acid (renal failure, calcium oxalate crystals)
• Both cause: elevated anion gap metabolic acidosis + elevated osmolal gap

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Early: elevated osmolal gap (parent alcohol); Late: elevated anion gap (toxic metabolites)
• Antidote: fomepizole (alcohol dehydrogenase inhibitor) — blocks conversion to toxic metabolites
• Indications for hemodialysis: severe acidosis, renal failure, visual symptoms (methanol), high levels

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Toxic Alcohol Ingestion:**

• Early: elevated osmolal gap (parent alcohol); Late: elevated anion gap (toxic metabolites)
• Antidote: fomepizole (alcohol dehydrogenase inhibitor) — blocks conversion to toxic metabolites
• Indications for hemodialysis: severe acidosis, renal failure, visual symptoms (methanol), high levels

mcqs.stem: A patient presents 2 hours after ingesting an unknown quantity of acetaminophen. Serum APAP level at 4 hours post-ingestion is 220 mcg/mL. Using the Rumack-Matthew nomogram, what is the appropriate management?

options.text: Start IV N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — the level is above the treatment line
options.explanation: On the Rumack-Matthew nomogram, the treatment line at 4 hours is 150 mcg/mL (FDA line) or 200 mcg/mL (original Rumack-Matthew line). At 220 mcg/mL, the level exceeds both thresholds. IV NAC (150 mg/kg loading, then 50 mg/kg over 4h, then 100 mg/kg over 16h) should be started immediately.

options.text: Administer activated charcoal only — NAC is not indicated within 4 hours of ingestion
options.explanation: Activated charcoal may be beneficial if given within 1-2 hours of ingestion, but it is NOT a substitute for NAC when the level indicates toxicity. NAC should be started as soon as possible.

options.text: Recheck level at 8 hours before deciding on treatment
options.explanation: With a clearly supratherapeutic level at 4 hours, treatment should begin immediately. Delaying NAC beyond 8 hours post-ingestion significantly increases risk of hepatotoxicity.

options.text: Obtain LFTs and treat only if transaminases are elevated
options.explanation: Hepatotoxicity from acetaminophen is delayed (ALT typically rises at 24-72 hours). Normal LFTs at 4 hours do not exclude impending liver injury. The nomogram guides treatment BEFORE liver damage occurs.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Start IV N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — the level is above the treatment line.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Start IV N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — the level is above the treatment line

coachDx.misconception: Hepatotoxicity from acetaminophen is delayed (ALT typically rises at 24-72 hours). Normal LFTs at 4 hours do not exclude impending liver injury. The nomogram guides treatment BEFORE liver damage occur
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Start IV N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — the level is above the treatment line from Obtain LFTs and treat only if transaminases are elevated.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Start IV N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — the level is above the treatment line.
